instructions.name: close_uninitialized_pool
docs: Admin-only: close a pool whose MPC callback never fired
(`is_initialized == false`).  Recovers the rent from pool_state, vault,
and nullifier_registry so `init_pool` can be retried cleanly.

accounts.name: vault
docs: Vault PDA — program-owned account that holds pooled SOL.
Owned by this program to enable direct lamport manipulation in withdraw
callback (no system_program::transfer CPI = no inner instruction log).

accounts.name: vault
docs: Vault PDA — program-owned, enabling direct lamport manipulation
without a system_program::transfer CPI (no inner instruction log).

errors.name: PoolNotInitialized
errors.msg: Pool not initialised — call init_pool first

errors.name: NoteNotReady
errors.msg: Note not in Ready status

errors.name: NoteNotFailed
errors.msg: Note is not in Failed status

errors.name: NoteNotWithdrawn
errors.msg: Note is not in Withdrawn status

errors.name: NoteAmountNotCleared
errors.msg: Note amount has not been cleared yet

errors.name: RecipientMismatch
errors.msg: Recipient does not match note

errors.name: ZeroAmount
errors.msg: Amount must be > 0

errors.name: PoolDenominationMismatch
errors.msg: Pool denomination does not match the note amount

errors.name: MpcFailed
errors.msg: Arcium MPC computation failed

errors.name: ClusterNotSet
errors.msg: Arcium cluster not set

errors.name: InsufficientVault
errors.msg: Vault has insufficient lamports for withdrawal

errors.name: InvalidNullifierRegistryOutput
errors.msg: Unexpected nullifier registry ciphertext shape

errors.name: InvalidWithdrawStatusCode
errors.msg: Unexpected withdraw status code from MPC output

errors.name: InvalidCircuitSourceOverride
errors.msg: Off-chain circuit override requires both source URL and 32-byte hash

errors.name: InvalidAuthority
errors.msg: Protocol authority must not be the default pubkey

errors.name: UnauthorizedProtocolAdmin
errors.msg: Only the protocol admin may perform this action

errors.name: UnauthorizedMaintenanceAuthority
errors.msg: Only the maintenance authority may perform this action

errors.name: NullifierAlreadySpent
errors.msg: Nullifier has already been spent

errors.name: InvalidNullifierRecord
errors.msg: Nullifier record does not match the provided note data

errors.name: InvalidNullifierHash
errors.msg: Nullifier hash must not be zero

errors.name: UnauthorizedRefundRecipient
errors.msg: Only the original sender may refund this failed deposit

errors.name: DepositAlreadyApplied
errors.msg: The deposit has already been credited to the encrypted pool balance

errors.name: NoRefundableLamports
errors.msg: No refundable lamports remain on this note

errors.name: PoolAlreadyInitialized
errors.msg: Pool is already initialized — cannot close

types.name: ClockAccount
docs: An account storing the current network epoch

types.name: CompactRegistryOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: ComputationDefinitionAccount
docs: An account representing a [ComputationDefinition] in a MXE.

types.name: ComputationDefinitionMeta
docs: A computation definition for execution in a MXE.

types.name: ComputationSignature
docs: The signature of a computation defined in a [ComputationDefinition].

types.name: DepositToPoolOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: Epoch
docs: The network epoch

types.name: InitPoolBalanceOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: MXEAccount
docs: A MPC Execution Environment.

types.name: MxeStatus
docs: The status of an MXE.

types.name: NodeMetadata
docs: location as [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html) country code

types.name: NodeRef
docs: A reference to a node in the cluster.
The offset is to derive the Node Account.
The current_total_rewards is the total rewards the node has received so far in the current
epoch.

types.name: NoteAccount
docs: Per-transfer note — PDA seeded by `[b"note", note_hash]`.
Lifecycle: `PendingMpc` → `Ready` → `Withdrawn` (transient) → closed
(or `Failed`).
**Privacy**:
- `encrypted_amount` is `Enc<Mxe, u64>` — only the MPC cluster can read it.
- `recipient_hash` is `SHA256(note_secret ∥ recipient_pubkey)` — the recipient
is hidden until withdrawal. No plaintext pubkey stored on-chain.
- `lamports_for_transfer` is plaintext but is set during deposit (when the
amount is already visible via the SOL CPI), NOT during withdraw.

fields.name: note_hash
docs: `SHA256(note_secret ∥ recipient ∥ amount_lamports_le)` — commitment
that reveals nothing without the 32-byte preimage.

fields.name: status
docs: Current lifecycle status of this note.

fields.name: sender
docs: Original sender. Used to authorize refunds when a deposit callback fails
before encrypted pool balance is credited.

fields.name: recipient_hash
docs: `SHA256(withdraw_key ∥ recipient_pubkey)` — hides the recipient until
withdrawal. The withdraw_key is a separate random value from note_secret,
ensuring that on-chain recipient verification reveals nothing about the
note secret or deposit linkage.

fields.name: nullifier_hash
docs: Public nullifier commitment derived from note_secret. Persisted outside
the encrypted registry so replay protection survives note closure and
nullifier-registry compaction.

fields.name: encrypted_amount
docs: `Enc<Mxe, u64>` — this note's encrypted amount. Set by deposit_callback.

fields.name: encrypted_secret_lo
docs: `Enc<Mxe, u128>` — lower 128 bits of the note secret, encrypted for MXE.
Used by the withdraw MPC circuit to verify secret ownership without
the plaintext secret ever appearing on-chain.

fields.name: encrypted_secret_hi
docs: `Enc<Mxe, u128>` — upper 128 bits of the note secret, encrypted for MXE.

fields.name: encrypted_pool_at_deposit
docs: `Enc<Mxe, u64>` — pool balance snapshot at element-0 of the DepositOutput
struct. Stored to preserve CTR-mode keystream positions when the full
struct is passed back to the withdraw circuit.

fields.name: amount_nonce
docs: Nonce for all `Enc<Mxe>` ciphertexts from the deposit callback output.

fields.name: pool_credit_applied
docs: Tracks whether the deposit callback successfully credited the encrypted
pool balance. Refunds are only allowed while this remains false.

fields.name: lamports_for_transfer
docs: Plaintext lamport amount for the SOL transfer. Set during deposit (when
the amount is already visible in the SOL CPI). Read by the withdraw
callback to execute the `vault → recipient` transfer.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

types.name: NoteStatus
docs: Note lifecycle status while the note account exists on-chain.

types.name: NullifierRegistryState
docs: Denomination-scoped encrypted nullifier registry.
The registry stores a bounded list of spent-nullifier words under MXE
encryption. The first word is the current entry count; the remaining words
are 2-limb (`u128`, `u128`) nullifier entries.

fields.name: encrypted_nullifiers
docs: Enc<Mxe, [u128; N]> flattened into ciphertext words.

fields.name: registry_nonce
docs: Nonce shared by every encrypted word in the registry output.

fields.name: pool_snapshot_ct
docs: Enc<Mxe, u64> — pool balance snapshot at element-0 of the struct output.
Stored to preserve CTR-mode keystream positions when passing the full
struct back to the withdraw circuit.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

types.name: Output
docs: An output of a computation.
We currently don't support encrypted outputs yet since encrypted values are passed via
data objects.

types.name: Parameter
docs: A parameter of a computation.
We differentiate between plaintext and encrypted parameters and data objects.
Plaintext parameters are directly provided as their value.
Encrypted parameters are provided as an offchain reference to the data.
Data objects are provided as a reference to the data object account.

types.name: PoolState
docs: Denomination-specific pool state — PDA seeded by `[b"pool", denomination_le]`.
`encrypted_balance` holds the running total of all deposited-minus-withdrawn
SOL as an `Enc<Mxe, u64>` ciphertext. Only the Arcium MPC cluster can
decrypt it. On-chain observers see 32 opaque bytes.

fields.name: encrypted_balance
docs: Enc<Mxe, u64> — the pool's encrypted running balance.

fields.name: balance_nonce
docs: Output nonce from the last MPC computation. Required to pass the
ciphertext back as an `Enc<Mxe>` input to the next computation.

fields.name: denomination_lamports
docs: Fixed lamport denomination routed through this pool.

fields.name: is_initialized
docs: Set to `true` by `init_pool_balance_callback`. Blocks deposits until
a valid `Enc<Mxe, 0>` is established.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump for the pool state account.

fields.name: vault_bump
docs: PDA bump for the vault account.

types.name: SetUnset
docs: Utility struct to store a value that needs to be set by a certain number of participants (keys
in our case). Once all participants have set the value, the value is considered set and we only
store it once.

types.name: VaultAccount
docs: Program-owned vault PDA seeded by `[b"vault", denomination_le]`.
Holds the pooled SOL. Because the account is owned by this program (not the
System Program), the withdraw callback can directly manipulate lamports
without a `system_program::transfer` CPI. This means no explicit transfer
instruction appears in the transaction's inner instruction logs.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

types.name: WithdrawFromPoolOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.
